skill.name: verification-before-completion
skill.description: Requires fresh verification evidence before claiming work is complete, fixed, passing, or ready for review.
skill.summary: Verify claims with current command output before reporting completion.
skill.author: Cycleaddict

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution, but these are documentation blocks, not executable code. Keyword hits for weak crypto and reconnaissance are also prose false positives. The skill does encourage running verification commands, so publication is safe with a low external-command risk note.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences and examples. They do not execute commands or define runnable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fenced Markdown text in SKILL.md. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command substitution syntax is present.

low_findings.title: Security Keyword Matches Without Malicious Context
low_findings.description: The weak crypto, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance hits are ordinary prose about verification behavior. No cryptography, host discovery, or network scanning is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text describes verification discipline and communication. I found no evidence of cryptographic logic, system probing, or network reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Broad Verification Command Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill asks the agent to identify and run verification commands. This is legitimate for coding work, but command execution should stay scoped to the active task.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs running verification commands, which can invoke external tools. The intent is quality control, and no malicious command or exfiltration path is present.

content.value_statement: AI coding agents can report success before checking current evidence. This skill makes Claude, Codex, and Claude Code verify work before completion claims.
actual_capabilities: Requires fresh verification before success or completion claims.
Guides agents to identify the command that proves each claim.
Prompts agents to read command output and exit status before reporting.
Encourages red-green verification for regression tests.
Adds checks before commits, pull requests, and task handoffs.
limitations: Does not provide project-specific test commands.
Does not guarantee that tests cover every requirement.
Requires tool access to run verification commands.
Cannot replace human review for product judgment.

use_cases.title: Pre-Commit Verification
use_cases.description: Confirm tests, linting, or builds before committing changes.

use_cases.title: Agent Task Review
use_cases.description: Check delegated agent work against actual diffs and verification output.

use_cases.title: Regression Confidence
use_cases.description: Verify that a bug fix includes a meaningful failing and passing test cycle.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Completion Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after making a small code change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Before saying this is complete, identify the command that proves it and run it now.

prompt_templates.title: Bug Fix Verification
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after fixing a reported defect.
prompt_templates.prompt: Verify the original bug symptom, run the relevant test, and report only what the output proves.

prompt_templates.title: Pull Request Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before opening or updating a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the diff, run the required verification commands, and list any unverified claims before PR creation.

prompt_templates.title: Regression Test Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a fix claims regression coverage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform red-green verification for the regression test and summarize the failing and passing evidence.

output_examples.input: The implementation is done. Can I report completion?
output_examples.output: Not yet. I need fresh verification output. I will run the relevant test command and report the result with evidence.

output_examples.input: The linter passed, so the build is ready.
output_examples.output: The linter result does not prove the build. I need to run the build command before making that claim.

output_examples.input: An agent said the task succeeded.
output_examples.output: I will inspect the diff and verify the changed behavior before accepting the agent report.

best_practices: Run the exact command that proves the claim.
Read the full output before summarizing status.
Report gaps honestly when verification is incomplete.
anti_patterns: Claiming success based on expected behavior.
Using old command output as current evidence.
Treating lint results as proof that builds or tests pass.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It prompts the agent to choose and run the correct verification command for the current task.

faq.question: Can it be used outside software projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It can apply to any work where a claim needs current evidence.

faq.question: Does it define the correct command for my project?
faq.answer: No. The agent must inspect the project and choose the relevant command.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low?
faq.answer: The skill encourages command execution for verification, but it does not include malicious commands or network behavior.

faq.question: Is it compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. It is intended for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What should happen when verification fails?
faq.answer: The agent should report the actual failure and avoid any completion claim.
